to whom, as well as to all others,
His arms are outstretched?


These brewers hotel keepers and
bartenders, 1t is only too plain, are
very largely in revolt against the
church Though l am not unmindful
Of the fact that a considerable pro-
portion of them are members of the
great Roman Catholic church, and
wherefore? Not, Irefuse to believe,
because, as alleged, that they are
concious of the nefarious character
Of their business, nor yet by reason
of the harmful nature of the infiu-
ence they are supposed to exert on
the community; but land of this l
am absolutely convinced) because
there have poured forth from a
thousand rostrums erected in places
dedicated to the preaching of the
gospel coarse denunciations, indis-
eliminate EXAMINATIONS directed
against a business supported by the
government, prosecuted by good and
loyal citizens, and only productive
of harm by reason of the inherent
weakness of human life and char.
acter


We speak with almost complete
knowledge of the situation and are
grieved at what I cannot but call
the unwisdom, to use no stronger
term, of those who profess to call
themselves Shristian preachers in
relation to the persons and business
under consideration. They misrep-
resent the spirit and the letter Of the
Christian faith, and are, to a large
degree, though, we hope, unintention-
be corrected seems to be part of the
responsibility which has fallen on
me.


Admitting that all members of
various so-called temperance organ
izations and clubs charge those who
make and sell alcoholic drinks with
being irresponsible sinnersand IL this
be true, then we have at once a sit
uation that calls for the exercise of
the most boundless Christian wis
dom that He came not to call the
righteous but sinners to repentance,
adding they that are whole need
not a physician, but they that are
sick"


1t came to my knowledge a short
time ago that when a honest young
man, a member of some Christian
organization, entered upon the care
Of a local hotel whose good will, etc,
he had purchased, his minister came
to him and declared that he must
not darken the door of the church
again until he had abandoned the
business of keeping a hotel.


Another of these ministers, I once
heard, refused to greet in a Masonic
lodge a gentleman owner of a SIM
